metadata.title: A Phase 2/3 Multicenter Study of Investigational Treatment in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors

cells.raw_value: To demonstrate that once-weekly tirzepatide 5 mg, and/or 10 mg, and/or 15 mg are superior to dulaglutide 0.75 mg in HbA1c change from baseline to 52 weeks in patients with T2DM who have discontinued OAM monotherapy or are OAM-naïve
Mean change in HbA1c from baseline at Week 52
To compare the efficacy of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks

Mean change in HbA1c
Proportion of patients who achieve HbA1c <7%, ≤6.5%, and <5.7%
Mean change in FSG
Mean change in daily average 7-point SMBG profiles
Mean change in body weight
Proportion of patients who achieve weight loss of ≥5%, ≥10%, and ≥15% from baseline
Mean change in fasting insulin
Mean change in fasting C-peptide
Mean change in HOMA-2

cells.raw_value:
Safety
• To compare the safety of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks

• Incidence of TEAEs
• Early discontinuations of investigational product due to AEs
• Adjudicated all deaths and nonfatal major CV events
• Adjudicated pancreatic AEs
• Serum calcitonin
• Incidence of allergic and hypersensitivity reactions
• Incidence of injection site reactions
• Incidence of treatment-emergent ADAs to tirzepatide
• The change in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, and heart rate from baseline
• Occurrence of hypoglycemic episodes
• Time to initiation of rescue therapy for severe persistent hyperglycemia

row_groups.name: Study Visit / Week of Treatment / Visit Window / Fasting Visit

resolved_footnotes: Visit 1 should be 4 weeks (OAM-naïve) or 10 weeks (at least 8-week OAM washout and extra 2 weeks before Visit 3) prior to Visit 3. For OAM washout, patients must complete the 8-week washout period prior to Visit 2.
ET visit is conducted within 14 days after the decision of study discontinuation.
Visit 801 should be 4 weeks after Visit 13 or 4 weeks after early termination.

Patients should be reminded to report to the site in a fasting condition, after a period of approximately 8 hours without eating, drinking (except water), or any significant physical activity and before taking investigational product.
The blood sample should be collected at Visit 3 before any investigational product has been given to the patient.
In the event of systemic drug hypersensitivity reactions (immediate or nonimmediate), additional blood samples will be collected including ADAs, PK, and exploratory immune safety sample.
PK samples for immunogenicity must be taken prior to drug administration.

footnotes.text: Visit 1 should be 4 weeks (OAM-naïve) or 10 weeks (at least 8-week OAM washout and extra 2 weeks before Visit 3) prior to Visit 3. For OAM washout, patients must complete the 8-week washout period prior to Visit 2.
ET visit is conducted within 14 days after the decision of study discontinuation.
Visit 801 should be 4 weeks after Visit 13 or 4 weeks after early termination.

Patients should be reminded to report to the site in a fasting condition, after a period of approximately 8 hours without eating, drinking (except water), or any significant physical activity and before taking investigational product.
The blood sample should be collected at Visit 3 before any investigational product has been given to the patient.
In the event of systemic drug hypersensitivity reactions (immediate or nonimmediate), additional blood samples will be collected including ADAs, PK, and exploratory immune safety sample.
PK samples for immunogenicity must be taken prior to drug administration.

cells.raw_value:
Primary
• To demonstrate that once-weekly tirzepatide 5 mg, and/or 10 mg, and/or 15 mg are superior to dulaglutide 0.75 mg in HbA1c change from baseline to 52 weeks in patients with T2DM who have discontinued OAM monotherapy or are OAM-naïve
• Mean change in HbA1c from baseline at Week 52

Secondary
Efficacy
• To compare the efficacy of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks

• Mean change in HbA1c
• Proportion of patients who achieve HbA1c <7%, ≤6.5%, and <5.7%
• Mean change in FSG
• Mean change in daily average 7-point SMBG profiles
• Mean change in body weight
• Proportion of patients who achieve weight loss of ≥5%, ≥10%, and ≥15% from baseline
• Mean change in fasting insulin
• Mean change in fasting C-peptide
• Mean change in HOMA-2

Safety
• To compare the safety of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks

• Incidence of TEAEs
• Early discontinuations of investigational product due to AEs
• Adjudicated all deaths and nonfatal major CV events
• Adjudicated pancreatic AEs
• Serum calcitonin
• Incidence of allergic and hypersensitivity reactions
• Incidence of injection site reactions
• Incidence of treatment-emergent ADAs to tirzepatide
• The change in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, and heart rate from baseline
• Occurrence of hypoglycemic episodes
• Time to initiation of rescue therapy for severe persistent hyperglycemia

resolved_footnotes: The starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, followed by an increase to 5 mg QW, for the duration of the study in the tirzepatide 5-mg arm. For the 10-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 mg) until the 10-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study. For the 15-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 to 12.5 to 15 mg) until the 15-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study.

The starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, followed by an increase to 5 mg QW, for the duration of the study in the tirzepatide 5-mg arm. For the 10-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 mg) until the 10-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study. For the 15-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 to 12.5 to 15 mg) until the 15-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study.

The starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, followed by an increase to 5 mg QW, for the duration of the study in the tirzepatide 5-mg arm. For the 10-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 mg) until the 10-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study. For the 15-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 to 12.5 to 15 mg) until the 15-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study.

footnotes.text: The starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, followed by an increase to 5 mg QW, for the duration of the study in the tirzepatide 5-mg arm. For the 10-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 mg) until the 10-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study. For the 15-mg arm, the starting dose of tirzepatide will be 2.5 mg QW for 4 weeks, then the dose will be increased by 2.5 mg every 4 weeks (2.5 to 5 to 7.5 to 10 to 12.5 to 15 mg) until the 15-mg dose is reached and maintained for the duration of the study.

procedures.raw_name:
Primary
• To demonstrate that once-weekly tirzepatide 5 mg, and/or 10 mg, and/or 15 mg are superior to dulaglutide 0.75 mg in HbA1c change from baseline to 52 weeks in patients with T2DM who have discontinued OAM monotherapy or are OAM-naïve

Safety
• To compare the safety of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks
procedures.canonical_name:
Safety
• To compare the safety of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks

procedures.raw_name:
Secondary
Efficacy
• To compare the efficacy of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks
procedures.canonical_name:
Secondary
Efficacy
• To compare the efficacy of once-weekly tirzepatide versus dulaglutide 0.75 mg at 52 weeks

procedures.raw_name:
Screening Lead-in
Washout OAM or D/E only
2 weeks or 8 weeks a
procedures.canonical_name:
Screening Lead-in
Washout OAM or D/E only
2 weeks or 8 weeks a
